context: The owner asked for a grounding layer for architect/implementer agents; the natural home .agents/doctrine/ is control-plane protected and the pre-tool hook denied a probe write.

options.option: .agents/doctrine/grounding/ via hook circumvention (MCP push or shell gaps)
options.rejected_because: deliberately routing around a repo guardrail violates the constitution's spirit even with owner intent

options.option: wait for the owner to unprotect the path
options.rejected_because: blocks delivery; protection is working as designed

selected: agent_docs/grounding/ — writable, already in AGENTS.md's required read-first corpus, and architecturally the advisory tier beneath enforced doctrine
trade_offs: Binding is softer: the library binds through read-first guidance, not through protected role files, until the owner applies the proposed role patch.

assumptions.assumption: AGENTS.md read-first item 6 gives sufficient pull-through for agents to actually load grounding files

decision_point: Grounding library shape: trigger-indexed domain files vs one big file vs expanding doctrine
context: ~900 concepts must be available without bloating every context window ('empirically useful, not a distraction').

options.rejected_because: all-or-nothing loading; ~3k lines in context per use
doctrine is enforced law and must stay small; awareness is not law

selected: 20 domain files + index with a decision→file trigger table, per-file design checklists, load discipline of 1-2 files per decision
trade_offs: Discoverability now depends on trigger-table accuracy; routing errors (found in dry-runs for webhooks and money types) silently hide guidance.

assumptions.assumption: Trigger-table routing is accurate enough after one adversarial dry-run pass

decision_point: Entry style: heuristics only, definitions banned
context: LLM readers already hold textbook definitions latently; file value must come from surfacing the right concern at decision time.

options.rejected_because: pure context cost; changes no decisions

selected: every entry states when the concept bites and the default move, ≤35 words, doctrine cross-referenced; critics rejected definition-only entries as findings
trade_offs: Terser entries assume competence; a junior human reader gets less scaffolding than a textbook would give.

assumptions.assumption: The checklist effect (naming the concern) is where LLM planning fails, not missing knowledge

context: The owner asked for iteration 'until acceptable'; unbounded loops burn budget without converging.

options.rejected_because: iteration count is not a quality signal

selected: stop when an independent fresh-context convergence verifier reports zero material findings (reached after: 4-lens critique → fixes → verify)
trade_offs: Usefulness was validated only by simulated dry-runs (4 feature scenarios), not by a live feature through the AERS loop; residual risk that real usage exposes routing gaps.

assumptions.assumption: Zero material findings from one fresh verifier approximates 'acceptable state'

context: The log must be diffable in PRs, appendable by any vendor's agent, and machine-checkable.

options.rejected_because: repo constitution: markdown is a view, never authority; free text drifts from any schema
ledger is runtime state outside git; the log must live in the PR diff where humans review

selected: append-only JSONL committed per feature pack (docs/decisions/ for non-feature work); one JSON object per line so each PR's diff is exactly its new decisions
trade_offs: Raw JSONL is less pleasant to read than markdown; mitigated by keeping entries short and skimmable in the diff.

assumptions.assumption: Reviewers will read JSONL lines in a diff without a rendered view

decision_point: Gate strictness: which entries require human validation before merge
context: Requiring human validation of every entry would make the gate a rubber stamp under volume; requiring none makes the log decorative.

options.rejected_because: review fatigue guarantees blind approval; Goodhart

options.option: no mechanical requirement, PR checkbox only
options.rejected_because: absence of review would be silent, violating the kit's evidence ethos

selected: mechanically require human validated/countered status only for one-way, low-confidence, or flagged-assumption entries; the rest may merge pending
trade_offs: Cheap reversible decisions can merge unreviewed; accepted because reversal is cheap and the record persists for later audit.

assumptions.assumption: one-way/low/flagged is the right risk cut for mandatory human attention

context: The decision-log gate needs risk tiers, exclusions, and the agent author id — all already defined for the independent-review gate.

options.rejected_because: two configs for one review policy drift apart; same tiers should gate both artifacts

trade_offs: Couples the two gates: retuning review tiers silently retunes decision-log tiers; acceptable while both express one review policy.

assumptions.assumption: Review tiers and decision-log tiers should always match

decision_point: How the protected control-plane files adopt the decision log
context: AGENTS.md workflow text and role files are protected; the log only fully binds agents once those files reference it.

options.option: edit protected files via GitHub API push, bypassing local hooks
options.rejected_because: same-run guardrail modification the constitution forbids; also DEC-TRI-001

selected: ship a git patch in docs/proposed-control-plane-change/ (the kit's designed channel) for the owner to apply under the dogfooding workflow
trade_offs: Until the owner applies the patch, the decision-log requirement is enforced by the gate but not stated in AGENTS.md, so an agent may discover it only at gate-failure time.

assumptions.assumption: Gate failure messages pointing at agent_docs/decision-log.md are a sufficient interim teaching mechanism

decision_point: Where to defend against gate self-relaxation
context: Red-team found both gates read policy (config.json) and contracts from the working tree, so a PR can un-gate itself; config.json and the gate files are agent-writable.

options.option: protect config.json only (control-plane patch), leave gate code trusting the working tree
options.rejected_because: protection is owner-applied and not yet active; the gate would still self-relax until then, and CI runs the branch's own gate code

options.option: rewrite gates to read policy/contract from an external ledger
options.rejected_because: over-engineered; the committed baseline (merge-base) is already the right immutable reference and needs no new store

selected: code-harden both gates to read policy and gated status from the committed baseline (merge-base with default branch), degrade safely to working tree with a loud note when no baseline resolves; AND ship the file-protection as a control-plane patch
trade_offs: Legitimate policy changes take effect only after merge, not within their own PR; accepted because that is correct for a security control (tightening later is fine, loosening needs review first).

assumptions.assumption: CI can make the base branch available (fetch or AERS_BASELINE_REF); documented as a CI note

decision_point: Append-only enforcement: what may change on an existing log entry
context: Nothing stopped an agent from silently rewriting or deleting prior decision-log lines; but humans legitimately edit entries to validate/counter them.

options.rejected_because: blocks the human review protocol, which sets human_status/validated_by/follow_up on existing entries

options.option: no append-only check, rely on PR diff review
options.rejected_because: silent rewrite of an inconvenient prior decision is exactly the tampering the log exists to prevent

selected: diff current log against the baseline: forbid deletion of any baseline entry and any change to immutable fields; permit only human_status, validated_by, follow_up to change
trade_offs: Correcting a genuinely wrong entry now requires a new entry referencing the old id rather than an in-place fix; matches the documented 'corrections are new entries' rule.

assumptions.assumption: human_status/validated_by/follow_up is the complete set of human-mutable fields

decision_point: How to handle unfixable red-team findings (humanity of validated_by, risk-label honesty, completeness, timing)
context: Several theater attacks are not mechanically detectable at acceptable false-positive cost; the docs previously overclaimed ('cannot merge without a named human's hand', 'unskippable').

options.option: add heuristic gate checks (timestamp clustering, sensitive-path vs cheap-claim) as hard failures
options.rejected_because: false-positive rate under squash-merge and broad sensitive_patterns would breed junk entries and erode trust in the gate

selected: correct the doc overclaims to state exactly what the gate guarantees, add a 'What the gate cannot see' limits section, and record RR-11..RR-13 in the residual-risk register; compensating control for each is CODEOWNERS + reviewer protocol
trade_offs: The mechanism is honestly weaker on paper than an overclaiming version, but the honesty is the point — a reviewer who trusts a false guarantee is worse off.

assumptions.assumption: documented residual + CODEOWNERS is the right disposition for humanity/completeness rather than a weak heuristic check
